title: Been working on a trading system for a while… but not sure how to move forward
author: Blitztradebot
selftext:
I’m not here to sell anything.

I’ve been working on a trading bot for a long time — testing it, breaking it, rebuilding it… over and over again.  
It’s based on a structured approach with controlled entries rather than random averaging, and I’ve backtested it across multiple market conditions since 2021.

Now I’m at a point where I want to turn this into something real — maybe even a small business.

But here’s the problem:  
I genuinely don’t know how to approach people with it.

Everywhere I look, it feels like people are either:

* aggressively selling bots
* or making unrealistic promises

And I don’t want to be that person.

I’d rather be honest and let people test it themselves — even with small amounts — and decide if it’s useful or not.

So I wanted to ask this community:

👉 If you were in my position, how would you approach people?  
👉 And from the other side — what would actually make *you* willing to try a new bot, even with a small amount?

Not looking for hype. Just real opinions.

I’d really appreciate any honest feedback.

comments.author: HappyThrasher99
comments.body: did you forget the part where you’re supposed to actually make money with it, considering they are supposed to make money

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body: Fair point. I'm not claiming it prints money — just trying to build something structured and see if it actually helps people over time.

comments.author: Strange-Pin-2717
comments.body: If your bot is making money, why sell ? And if it ain't making money then why sell to gullible new traders.

replies.author: zoetsekas
replies.body: well you can use it to make money from it, but selling subscriptions as well will accelerate your profits and you can put that money to reinvest

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
Yeah, I completely understand why that sounds suspicious.  
  
I'm not trying to sell a "profit machine."  
  
I built it primarily for myself first, and it's something I actually use.  
What I'm trying to figure out now is whether it can be useful for others too.  
  
If it doesn't provide real value, people shouldn't use it — I agree with that.

replies.author: pr0XYTV
replies.body: people usually sell things to make more money

comments.author: zoetsekas
comments.body: I am doing the same, created LLC, built web site, currently deploying to cloud, setup 3rd party integrations... it's tough, but keep moving forward

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
Appreciate this a lot.

Yeah, it's honestly been way harder than I expected too — especially the part where you move from building to actually getting people to trust it. Respect for going through the same process. Out of curiosity, how are you thinking about distribution or getting your first users?

replies.author: zoetsekas
replies.body: I was thinking about the various channels, initially as a web portal (B2C), need some organic growth and some marketing. I am trying not to get to trouble with SEC, so need to stay in my lane, unless I get certified to provide investment advice. B2B, selling services to RIAs or other institutions is another choice, lastly social trading, see etoro for copyTrader. Many avenues, but need a complete product first

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
yeah that makes a lot of sense  
  
the SEC part is something I’ve been thinking about too tbh  
  
feels like there are a ton of paths, but yeah… probably just need to focus on making the product actually solid first  
  
curious — are you building more on the automation side or signal/copy trading?

comments.author: Different-Scholar269
comments.body: How about you trade it. Hook your broker to myfxbook, so it's public. Do some prop firm challenges. Show it makes money. Good results sell them self

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
That’s actually really solid advice.  
  
I’ve been thinking about making the performance more transparent, just haven’t figured out the best way to do it yet.  
Appreciate this — it makes a lot of sense.

comments.author: Automatic-Essay2175
comments.body:
It sounds like you have absolutely no idea what you’re doing. You’ve been working on one system? Just one? For how long? I had to build about 80 different ‘bots’ over the course of several years before I found something that works extremely well. And by the time I got there, I had absolutely no interest in selling or sharing it because I actually understood the liquidity limitations and scalability of my system. 

It doesn’t seem like you’ve thought about this. It doesn’t sound like you’ve put the work in. It doesn’t sound like your system even makes money. 

I’m telling you this because I was in your position many years ago. Let me save you some time. No one wants to use your bot. Go back to work, try to actually make some money with an automated system. If you manage to put in the time and effort to actually get there, I promise you’ll look back on this post and wonder what the fuck you were thinking.

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
Fair enough — I get where you're coming from.  
  
Just trying to figure things out step by step. Thank you for your opinion btw.

comments.author: One-View-8330
comments.body: Just crypto or stocks too? What fees how much do you have it running (money wise). How have does it make?

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
Fair questions.  
  
Right now it's focused on crypto only.  
There’s no cost to use it.  
The system runs through specific exchange accounts tied to my referral setup — that’s how I plan to monetize it instead of charging users directly.  
  
In terms of capital, I’ve been running it with a modest amount in a controlled setup.  
The focus so far has been on execution logic, risk management, and how it behaves across different market conditions.  
  
As for returns — I’m not comfortable throwing numbers out yet.  
I’d rather make that properly transparent first (e.g. public tracking) once everything is finalized.  
  
Still early stage — just trying to build something that actually holds up over time.

comments.author: RevanVar1
comments.body: Guys, the entire post is made by ai and all of his responses are ai 😂 don’t get bated by the scammers lol

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
lol nah not AI.  
just overexplaining things I guess

replies.author: RevanVar1
replies.body: You do realize all anyone has to do is throw your comments into an ai checker lmao 😂 bro you don’t even have your gpt take out its dashes 😂

comments.author: zoetsekas
comments.body: My product will publish regularly stock portfolios as initial products. No automation because of SEC rules, can't provide bespoke services. I will provide API and websockets so clients create their own automation to their portfolios. More paths forward later on

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
that’s actually a pretty clean setup  
API + letting users handle automation makes sense with the SEC stuff  
been thinking about similar constraints tbh  
are you planning to stick with portfolios long term or move closer to execution later?

comments.author: zoetsekas
comments.body: need a solid foundation first, operating costs are ramping up

replies.author: Blitztradebot
replies.body:
yeah same here tbh  
  
costs add up quick  
just trying to get the foundation right before anything else

comments.author: ReceptionUnlucky9455
comments.body: I built a completely private ninjatrader prop trading system particularly for trading prop accounts. It learns the prop  firm rules and let you set strategy based on that. It paused or skips trading if event trading is not allowed to some accounts. It flattens all every day 40 mins before close time … let me know if you are interested to test, I will share the link

comments.author: eggrally
comments.body: Bots will run the marketnin 6 months, every one is vibe coding.  I have a bot that has a 100% win rate, can't share it , I'm the new Renaissance Fund

comments.author: Optimal_Emu3624
comments.body: You better hurry, pretty soon no humans will be pressing the “Buy” button on their crypto exchange app. The Agents are coming. I built a 1 over 7 agentic eval/trade execution system as well, took about three months. Trading spot and margins live on Kraken. I have a mobile app almost finished also that will be able to monitor the system in real time, and change strategy on the fly as well. Web app, and mobile. Now all I need to do is get a bag of money to deposit into my kraken and let er rip. Been paper trading for three days now. I am hoping us little guys can lock down the agent trading space before it’s completely taken over by Wall Street. I built it so that is a deliverable also, just haven’t thought about marketing.

comments.author: CheetahOtherwise4861
comments.body:
Hola.
Llevo 15 años en el nicho.
Mis consejos:
-Migra desde cuenta Demo a cuenta Cent.
-Crea una auditoria de resultados en fxblue.
-Con minimo 3 meses de resultados, puedes usar esa auditoria para fines comerciales.
-Realiza la documentación del bot, tipo manual de operacioes, para el usiario final.
-Haz un video haciendo backtest de la estrategia y explicando sus métricas.
-Crea una landing page donde insertes toda la información, incluyendo el video y la auditoría.
-Crea una comumidad publica en telegram para resolver preguntas y publicar resultados.
-Crea un modulo de licencias para el bot.
-Dostribuye una licencia Demo por tiempo limitado.
-Sé claro con los riesgos y no vendas humo.
-Asociate con un broker. Maneja bajos precios y apalancate del IB program del broker.
-Y lo más dificil: Da soporte constante y sé persistente.

comments.author: pr0XYTV
comments.body:
its not easy but can be done.  
i had to constantly publish my results with my trading program before I was able to attract a client.  
Everyone thinks just because you have a functional system you would have billions printing from it, thats just not the case lol.  
My client knows this and Ive also made myself useful in other ways by creating them custom software and being available like a broker in exchange for weekly retainer.

Good luck!

comments.author: Forsaken_Leader_8
comments.body: honestly, the best way to move forward without looking like a scam is to just open a free beta. tell people you aren't looking for money, just for users to run it on a paper account or a tiny "coffee money" sub-account and give you brutal feedback. i’ve seen builders succeed by just posting a weekly log of the bot’s actual performance, including the losses. i personally use signalwhisper to handle the high-level research layer for my own setups, and i only trust tools that show the "why" behind the logic. if you show the raw data and let people test it for free, the trust builds itself.

comments.author: Cultural_Chapter_292
comments.body: Just connect your acc to myFxbook or ctrader and show that your bot makes money, there are people who just want to invest and have their money work for them without looking at charts. You get a profit split and set the minimum of how much it is for people to join and copy your trades 😏
